Asian American

Movement Archive
About History Ask the OGs Browse the Collection

AAPA contingent at Black Panther Party, Free Huey Rally, Alameda County Courthouse. Oakland, Summer 1968.

VWong AAPA Archive

Historic protest march by AAPA with community activists against the 'Glittering Ghetto' of Chinatown conditions and exploitation of immigrant and lower-income workers in the Summer of 1968.

VWong AAPA Archive

Thousands of protesters from around the Bay Area rushed to SF Chinatown/Manilatown and ringed what was called "the Red Block" to fight the cowardly eviction of the mainly elderly tenants of the International Hotel (I-Hotel) by SFPD on Aug. 4, 1977. The nearly decade-long struggle for low-income housing, saving Manilatown and community needs over corporate greed was another battle front that AAPA and the TWLF had sparked and broadened.

VWong AAPA Archive

Third World Liberation Front (TWLF), which AAPA cofounded, led the Third World Strike at UC Berkeley from Jan.-Mar. 1969, that won the first Ethnic Studies and Asian American Studies entities in the U.S. Above, TW strikers are blocking Sather Gate, the main entrance to campus.

VWong AAPA Archive

AAPA greatly radicalized the antiwar movement by targeting US imperialism in Asia, as above leading a massive protest in SF in 1969.

Unknown

Kearny St. entrances to Everybody's Bookstore & ACC.

VWong AAPA Archive

A living archive dedicated to the tradition of radical, internationalist Asian American and Third World Liberation Front (TWLF) organizing, rooted in self-determination, "Serve the People" community programs, and working-class solidarity.
